title: Godalming Taxis | 24hr Local Cabs | Chiddingfold Taxis
metaDescription: Local Godalming taxis — station runs, airport transfers and local journeys, 24/7. Fixed fare agreed before you travel. Call 01483 387 475.
intro: Godalming is one of the runs we do most. We're based a few miles south in Chiddingfold, which keeps us local to the whole GU7 and GU8 patch rather than driving out from Guildford or Woking. Chiddingfold Taxis covers Godalming, Farncombe and the villages around them, 24 hours a day, seven days a week. Call 01483 387 475 and we will agree a fixed fare before you travel.

sections.heading: Local to Godalming, not driving out from town
sections.body:
Plenty of the cars you can book in this town drive in from Guildford, Woking or further out, and you wait while they do it. We're based in Chiddingfold, a few miles south — close enough to be genuinely local to Godalming, and not fighting our way across the county to reach you. That matters most on the jobs where minutes count: a 5am pickup in Busbridge, a train you have twelve minutes to catch, a lift home from the Royal Surrey when you have been discharged later than expected.

It also means our drivers know the place the way locals do rather than the way a satnav does. Which roads clog at school-run time. That the one-way system around Flambard Way is not intuitive the first time. Which end of a long lane in Binscombe or up on Frith Hill your front door is actually on.

sections.heading: Godalming and Farncombe station runs
sections.body:
Godalming station is on the Portsmouth Direct line, about 34 miles down from London Waterloo, with direct trains taking roughly 40 to 45 minutes and usually two or three an hour. Farncombe, a mile or so north, sits on the same line and is a similar run into Waterloo. Neither is any fun to walk to at six on a February morning with a suitcase.

Station work is our bread and butter: the early commuter run, the pickup when the last train down from Waterloo drops you at Godalming after midnight, and the reverse, when someone arrives into Farncombe needing a lift out to Hambledon or Wormley. We take calls 24 hours a day, so a delayed train is not a problem. We would just ask you to ring us once you know the new time, rather than assume we are watching the board.

sections.heading: Everyday runs around the town
sections.body:
Most of what we do here is local and unglamorous, which is rather the point.

  • Schools and colleges — runs to Godalming College, Charterhouse and the primaries, plus the pickups when work overruns.
  • Shopping — the High Street and the Pepperpot end of town, or a bigger shop where the boot earns its keep. Ours is a red Toyota Corolla saloon with a large boot that swallows a fortnight's shopping or two big cases without an argument.
  • Hospital and surgery — the Royal Surrey in Guildford is roughly six miles up the A3100, usually fifteen to twenty minutes depending on traffic.
  • Evenings out — pubs and restaurants in town or over in Guildford, with a car home at whatever hour you finish.
  • Courier — same-day parcels and documents when something has to be there today.

sections.heading: A town worth knowing your way around
sections.body: The Pepperpot has stood at the top of the High Street since 1814, and the plaque underneath it marks the world's first public electricity supply, switched on here on 26 September 1881 from a waterwheel at Westbrook Mill on the Wey. Not many towns can say that.

sections.heading: Airports and further afield
sections.body:
Airport transfers are our main trade. From Godalming, Heathrow is roughly 28 miles and typically somewhere around 45 minutes to an hour, traffic allowing. Gatwick is a similar distance but usually a slower drive, so we would allow an hour or more. Luton, Stansted and London City are longer again, as are Southampton and Portsmouth for cruise departures.

Those times are approximate and entirely traffic-dependent. We will talk through a sensible pickup time when you book, and we would rather set off early than let the M25 make the decision for us. We cover all Heathrow terminals, T2 to T5, and both Gatwick terminals. Tell us which one when you book.

sections.heading: Prices and booking
sections.body: We do not publish a price list, because a fare depends on where you are going, when, and how many of you there are. Ring 01483 387 475 or WhatsApp 07453 267475, tell us the job, and we will agree a fixed fare before you travel. Nothing is metered, so nothing creeps up in traffic. Email info@chiddingfoldtaxis.co.uk if that is easier.

faqs.q: Is there a 24 hour taxi in Godalming?
faqs.a: Yes. We answer the phone 24 hours a day, seven days a week, on 01483 387 475 — nights, weekends and bank holidays included. For a 4am airport run it is worth booking ahead where you can, but we take calls round the clock.

faqs.q: How much is a taxi from Godalming station?
faqs.a: It depends where you are going, so we do not publish fixed prices. Call or WhatsApp us with the destination and we will agree a fixed fare before you travel and stick to it. Nothing is metered.

faqs.q: How long does it take to get from Godalming to Heathrow by taxi?
faqs.a: Roughly 45 minutes to an hour for the 28 or so miles, but that is an approximation and entirely traffic-dependent — the M25 can add a lot. We plan pickup times with a margin built in rather than promise a journey time.

faqs.q: Do you cover Farncombe and the villages around Godalming?
faqs.a: Yes. Farncombe, Busbridge, Binscombe, Ockford Ridge, Charterhouse, Frith Hill and Crownpits are everyday runs for us, and we go out to Milford, Witley, Chiddingfold, Hambledon, Haslemere and the villages beyond.

faqs.q: Can I book a Godalming taxi in advance?
faqs.a: Yes, and for early airport runs and station pickups we would encourage it. Ring 01483 387 475 with the date, time, pickup address and destination — plus the terminal, if it is an airport.

title: Airport Transfers from Godalming | Chiddingfold Taxis
metaDescription: Airport transfers from Godalming & Chiddingfold to Heathrow, Gatwick, Luton, Stansted & London City. 24/7, fixed fare up front. Call 01483 387 475.
h1: Airport Transfers from Godalming, Chiddingfold and across Surrey
intro:
Airport work is the bulk of what we do. We're based in Chiddingfold, which puts us right among the GU8 villages and a short run from the A3 at Milford, with Godalming and Haslemere both a few miles away — so a 4am pickup means a local driver setting off from down the road, not one crawling out from London.

We run 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, and the fare is agreed before you travel. Call 01483 387 475 for a price.

sections.heading: Every London airport, plus the south coast cruise ports
sections.body:
Distances below are measured from the Godalming/Chiddingfold area. Times are approximate and depend entirely on traffic — the M25 in particular can add half an hour without warning.

  • Heathrow — about 27 miles, typically 45–60 minutes. Usually A3 north to the M25 at Junction 10 (Wisley), then round to J15 and the M4 for Terminals 2 and 3, or J14 for Terminals 4 and 5. More on our Heathrow transfers.
  • Gatwick — about 28 miles, typically 50–70 minutes. Cross-country on the A281 towards Horsham, then the A264 and M23. More on our Gatwick transfers.
  • Luton — about 60 miles, typically 1 hr 20 – 1 hr 45. A3 to the M25, clockwise to J21, then the M1 north to Junction 10.
  • Stansted — about 70 miles, typically 1 hr 40 – 2 hrs. The long way round the M25 to J27, then the M11 north to Junction 8.
  • London City — about 43 miles, typically 1 hr 20 – 1 hr 45. Either straight up the A3 and through town, or M25 and the Dartford Crossing depending on the hour.
  • Southampton cruise terminals — about 50 miles, usually a little over the hour via the A31 and M3/M27.
  • Portsmouth International Port — about 40 miles, usually 50–70 minutes straight down the A3 through the Hindhead tunnel.

sections.heading: Tell us the terminal, not just the airport
sections.body:
Heathrow has four working terminals — T2 (the Queen's Terminal), T3, T4 and T5. Terminal 1 closed years ago and no longer exists. They're spread across the airport and approached from different junctions, so knowing which one you need before we set off saves a genuinely annoying loop round the perimeter road.

Gatwick has North and South Terminals, separated by the runway and linked by a free shuttle. Airlines do move between them — easyJet consolidated into North in 2026, for instance — so please check your boarding pass rather than going on what was true last time you flew. If you're not sure, tell us the airline and flight number when you book and we'll work it out with you.

sections.heading: How early should you leave?
sections.body:
This is guidance, not a promise — you know your own flight and check-in better than we do. As a rough starting point, most people work back from the airport using the airline's advice (commonly around 2 hours before a short-haul departure and 3 hours for long-haul), then add the drive time above, then add a buffer.

Things worth padding for:

  • Weekday mornings, roughly 6.30–9.30am. The A3 northbound and the M25 both bite. We'd add 30–45 minutes for a Heathrow run in that window.
  • Friday afternoons heading anywhere near the M25.
  • Bank holidays and half-term Saturdays to Gatwick.
  • Roadworks and closures — the A3 and M25 both get overnight closures with diversions that add real time.

Ring us and we'll talk through a sensible pickup time for your specific flight. We'd always rather you sat in departures with a coffee than watched the clock in traffic.

sections.heading: Luggage and the car
sections.body: The car is a red Toyota Corolla saloon with a large boot — it takes a family's worth of hold luggage without anyone travelling with a case on their lap. Golf clubs, a pushchair, a cello, three weeks of cruise luggage: tell us what's coming when you book and we'll be straight with you about whether it fits. If it genuinely doesn't, we'd rather say so up front than turn up and play Tetris on your driveway at 5am.

sections.heading: Early starts, late landings and the run home
sections.body:
Plenty of Surrey airport work happens at hours when nothing else is moving. We cover it: 3am departures from Chiddingfold, 1am landings at Gatwick, Sunday nights, Christmas morning. Twenty-four hours, every day.

For return journeys, give us your flight number and landing time and we'll agree a pickup arrangement with you when you book — including where to meet and what happens if you're delayed. It's worth booking the return at the same time as the outbound, especially for early-morning arrivals.

sections.heading: A fixed fare, agreed before you travel
sections.body:
We don't publish price lists, because a Godalming–Heathrow run at 5am and a Chiddingfold–Stansted run on a bank holiday aren't the same job. What we do instead is simpler: you call, you tell us where from, where to, when and how many of you, and we agree a fixed fare there and then. That's the price. It doesn't move because the M25 was slow.

Call 01483 387 475, WhatsApp 07453 267475, or email info@chiddingfoldtaxis.co.uk.

faqs.q: How long does it take to get from Godalming to Heathrow?
faqs.a: Usually somewhere between 45 minutes and an hour, covering about 27 miles via the A3 and M25. It's traffic-dependent and we can't guarantee it — a weekday morning peak can add another 30–45 minutes, so we'd build that in when we agree your pickup time.

faqs.q: How much is a taxi from Godalming to Gatwick?
faqs.a: We don't publish fixed price lists, because the fare depends on the exact pickup point, the time of day and how many passengers there are. Call 01483 387 475 and we'll agree a fixed fare with you before you travel, so you know the price up front.

faqs.q: Do you do early morning airport runs from Chiddingfold?
faqs.a: Yes — we run 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, and early departures from the GU8 villages are routine for us. We're based in Chiddingfold, a few miles from Godalming, so a 3.30am pickup is a local driver coming to you.

faqs.q: Which Heathrow terminal do I need?
faqs.a: Heathrow has Terminals 2, 3, 4 and 5 (Terminal 1 is long closed). Your terminal is on your boarding pass or booking confirmation — British Airways mostly uses T5. Tell us the airline and flight number when you book and we'll confirm it with you.

faqs.q: Can you take us to Southampton or Portsmouth for a cruise?
faqs.a: Yes. Southampton's cruise terminals are around 50 miles away, usually a bit over an hour; Portsmouth International Port is around 40 miles, typically 50–70 minutes down the A3. The boot takes cruise luggage — tell us how many cases when you call.

title: Godalming to Heathrow Taxi | 24/7 | Chiddingfold Taxis
metaDescription: Taxi to Heathrow from Godalming. Local team, 24/7, all terminals. Fixed fare agreed before you travel. Call 01483 387 475.
intro: Heathrow is where most Godalming airport journeys end up, and it's the run we do most. It's roughly 25 to 30 miles up the A3 and round the M25 — usually somewhere around 40 to 50 minutes when the roads behave, and longer when they don't. We're a local team based in Chiddingfold, running 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, with a fixed fare agreed before you travel. Call 01483 387 475 for a quote.

sections.heading: How far is Heathrow from Godalming?
sections.body:
Godalming sits roughly 25 to 30 miles from Heathrow, depending on which terminal you need — Terminal 5 is the nearest side of the airport, and the central terminals a little further round. With clear roads, the drive is usually somewhere around 40 to 50 minutes.

That's an approximation rather than a promise. The A3 and the M25 are both busy roads, and the same journey can take half an hour longer at the wrong time of day. We plan every airport run with a sensible buffer built in, rather than betting on a clear road.

sections.heading: The route we take
sections.body:
Almost every Heathrow run from Godalming starts the same way: north on the A3 towards Guildford, then on up to the M25 at junction 10 — the Wisley interchange, where the A3 meets the motorway — and clockwise round to the Heathrow junctions.

National Highways spent several years rebuilding junction 10, adding a larger roundabout and free-flow left turns at the corners. The main parts of that are now open, though the area has seen a great deal of disruption and it still pays to keep an eye on it. If the A3 or the M25 is genuinely blocked, our drivers know the local alternatives — that comes from driving these roads every day, not from following a satnav blindly.

sections.heading: Which terminal? It changes the last few miles
sections.body:
Tell us your terminal when you book.

  • Terminal 5 — signed from the M25 at junction 14, or the M4 at junction 4b. It's the closest part of the airport to us.
  • Terminals 2 and 3 — the central terminal area in the middle of the airport, reached from the M4 at junction 4 or the M25 at junction 15.
  • Terminal 4 — M25 junction 14, then the A3113 (Airport Way) and the Southern Perimeter Road, over on the south side.

T4 and T5 sit on opposite sides of Heathrow, so a wrong guess at the airport can cost you twenty minutes you hadn't planned for. Heathrow also charges its own drop-off fee for vehicles entering the terminal forecourts (£7 at the time of writing — worth checking heathrow.com for the current figure).

sections.heading: Traffic: an early start is a different journey
sections.body:
The two reliable pressure points on this route are Guildford and the M25 itself. Northbound on the A3 on a weekday morning, traffic around the A31 merge at Guildford tails back — on bad mornings, back towards Milford. And junction 10 at Wisley carries over 300,000 vehicles a day; National Highways describes it as the busiest section of the M25.

A 4am airport run is a completely different journey from a 7:30am one. Early starts are usually clear and quick. Leave between roughly seven and nine on a weekday and you're in the thick of it, and late afternoon is the other one to watch. That's why we ask what time your flight is, rather than only what time you'd like collecting.

sections.heading: Working out when to leave
sections.body:
We can't tell you when your airline wants you there — but we can help you work backwards.

Heathrow's own advice is to get to the airport three hours before an international flight, or two hours if you're flying domestically or to Europe. Your airline will have its own check-in and bag-drop deadlines on top of that, and those are usually the real cut-off, so check them first.

Then allow for the drive — about an hour from Godalming is a sensible working figure at a quiet time, more in rush hour — plus a margin on top. Tell us your flight time and terminal on the phone and we'll talk it through and agree a pickup that gives you room. It's guidance based on driving this route constantly, not a guarantee: traffic is traffic, and we won't pretend otherwise.

sections.heading: Luggage, early starts, returns and the fare
sections.body:
Our car is a red Toyota Corolla saloon with a large boot — genuinely large, which matters when it's two big cases, a cabin bag and coats. If you're a full car with a lot of luggage, tell us when you ring so we can check it'll all fit.

We run 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, so a 3:30am pickup for a first-wave departure is normal work rather than a favour. We'll cover the return leg from Heathrow back to Godalming too — easiest to book both on the same call.

We don't publish fares, because the honest answer depends on the terminal, the time and the job. Ring us and we'll agree a fixed fare before you travel, so the number you're quoted is the number you pay.

faqs.q: How long does a taxi take from Godalming to Heathrow?
faqs.a: Roughly 40 to 50 minutes when the roads are clear — it's about 25 to 30 miles, depending on your terminal. In weekday rush hour, or if the A3 or M25 is having a bad day, it can take considerably longer. We treat those as approximate, traffic-dependent figures and build in a buffer rather than promising a time.

faqs.q: How much is a taxi from Godalming to Heathrow?
faqs.a: We don't publish fares. Ring us on 01483 387 475 with your terminal, date and pickup time, and we'll agree a fixed fare before you travel — so you know the price up front and it doesn't change on the day.

faqs.q: What time should I leave Godalming for a Heathrow flight?
faqs.a: Start with your airline's check-in and bag-drop deadlines, plus Heathrow's own advice — three hours before an international flight, two hours if you're flying domestically or to Europe. Then allow for the drive (about an hour from Godalming is a sensible working figure at a quiet time, more at busy times) and add a margin. Tell us the flight time and terminal and we'll talk it through and agree a pickup. That's guidance from driving the route, not a guarantee.

faqs.q: Do you do early morning taxis to Heathrow from Godalming?
faqs.a: Yes. We run 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, so a 3am or 4am pickup from Godalming for a first-wave Heathrow departure is routine for us. Early runs are usually the quickest of the day, before the A3 and M25 fill up.

faqs.q: Can you pick us up from Heathrow when we fly back?
faqs.a: Yes — we cover return trips from Heathrow to Godalming, from any terminal. It's easiest to book the outbound and the return on the same call, so we've got your flight details and terminal on file.

title: Godalming to Gatwick Taxi | Chiddingfold Taxis
metaDescription: Taxi to Gatwick from Godalming, 24 hours. Fixed fare agreed before you travel, North and South Terminal, local drivers. Call 01483 387 475.
intro: Gatwick is our busiest run out of Godalming: early starts, late pick-ups and everything between. It is roughly 28 to 30 miles depending on which way we go, and the honest answer on timing is that it depends on the hour you travel. Here is how we actually drive it, and how to work out when to leave.

sections.heading: The route: A281 through Bramley, Cranleigh and Horsham
sections.body:
The default line is north on the A3100 to Shalford, then south-east on the A281 Horsham Road through Bramley, Grafham, Alfold and Bucks Green, on through Broadbridge Heath and round Horsham. From the Roffey side the A264 runs east past Faygate to the M23 at Pease Pottage, then it is a couple of junctions north to junction 9 and the Gatwick spur.

Shortest in miles, and a lovely drive, but single carriageway nearly all the way. Shalford and Bramley are the pinch points — the A281 squeezes through both villages and queues badly at school-run time and again from late afternoon.

When it is sitting still we leave on the B2130 instead, out past Winkworth Arboretum and through Hascombe to Cranleigh, rejoining the A281 near Alfold. It skips Shalford and Bramley altogether. Narrow in places, so not always faster, but knowing when to use it is the difference between a calm run and a scramble.

sections.heading: The motorway way round: A3, M25, M23
sections.body:
The alternative goes the long way: A3 north to the Wisley interchange at M25 junction 10, clockwise to Merstham at junction 7, then south on the M23 to junction 9. More miles, but dual carriageway and motorway throughout.

We take it for very early flights, when the M25 is quiet and the villages have not woken up, and whenever the A281 is blocked — one accident between Bramley and Shalford shuts that road for hours with no quick way round. In the morning peak the sums flip, and we would rather trickle through Cranleigh than sit still on the M25. We check the traffic before setting off and choose on the day.

sections.heading: North Terminal or South Terminal
sections.body:
The terminals are a couple of miles apart and you cannot walk between them, so it matters. Both come off the junction 9 spur: South is signposted from the airport roundabout, North has its own access road off it.

Broadly, easyJet and much of the long-haul traffic use North; British Airways, TUI and Vueling use South. Airlines do move, so trust the terminal on your boarding pass rather than last time's memory. Tell us when you book and we will drop you at the right door.

sections.heading: When to leave for your flight
sections.body:
Gatwick asks you to arrive two hours before a European flight, three before long-haul, and check-in closes 45 minutes before short-haul departures and 60 before long-haul. Work backwards from that, not from take-off.

Rough guidance only, because traffic decides this and not us:

  • Before 8am: roads as good as they get. Allow about an hour door to terminal on top of your check-in window.
  • Mid-morning to mid-afternoon: the quiet window, an hour to an hour and a quarter.
  • Weekday peaks, roughly 7 to 9.30am and 4 to 7pm: add a good half hour, often more. Shalford, Bramley and the M25 all bite at once.
  • Fridays and the start of the school holidays: treat every estimate as optimistic.

We will not promise a journey time, because nobody honestly can. We build the margin in and turn up early.

sections.heading: Coming home, and what it costs
sections.body:
Give us your flight number and terminal and we will sort out where to meet you. Landing at 1am is fine; we run 24 hours.

We do not publish prices, because a Gatwick fare depends on your address, the terminal, the time and how many are travelling. Ring or WhatsApp with those details and we will agree a fixed fare before you travel, so the price we quote is the price you pay. The car is a red Toyota Corolla saloon with a large boot. We are a small team of friendly local drivers based in Chiddingfold.

faqs.q: How long does a taxi from Godalming to Gatwick take?
faqs.a: About 28 to 30 miles by road. An early run is often around 50 minutes to an hour; in the weekday peak it can be an hour and a half or more. Approximate and traffic-dependent, always.

faqs.q: How much is a taxi from Godalming to Gatwick Airport?
faqs.a: We do not publish fixed prices. Call 01483 387 475 with your address, terminal, time and numbers, and we will agree a fixed fare before you travel.

faqs.q: Is it quicker to get the train from Godalming to Gatwick?
faqs.a: There is no direct train. You change at Guildford onto the Great Western service via Redhill, a bit over an hour before you have got to the station or waited for a connection. With luggage or an early flight, door to terminal is simpler.

faqs.q: What time should I be picked up for a 6am flight?
faqs.a: Check-in closes 45 minutes before a short-haul departure and Gatwick asks you there two hours ahead, which points to roughly 3.15 to 3.45am. Tell us the flight and we will suggest a time.

faqs.q: Do you cover Gatwick North Terminal as well as South?
faqs.a: Yes, both, in both directions. Tell us which is on your boarding pass.

title: Haslemere Taxis | 24/7 Local Cabs | Chiddingfold Taxis
metaDescription: Friendly local taxis in Haslemere GU27 — station runs, airport transfers, 24 hours. Fixed fare agreed before you travel. Call 01483 387 475.
h1: Haslemere Taxis — Local Cars, 24 Hours a Day
intro: We're Chiddingfold Taxis, and we cover Haslemere and the GU27 villages every day of the year. Our base is in Chiddingfold, a few miles away across the GU8 lanes. and we answer the phone at any hour: 01483 387 475.

sections.heading: Haslemere station runs, morning and night
sections.body:
Haslemere station sits on the Portsmouth Direct line, run by South Western Railway. Northbound there are around three trains an hour to London Waterloo via Guildford and Woking, and the quickest services do it in a little under the hour, though the stopping trains take noticeably longer. Southbound you can pick up Petersfield, Liphook, Havant and Portsmouth Harbour, with some morning and evening trains reaching Southampton Central and Fareham.

The station has a decked car park, but if you'd rather not leave the car sitting there all week, we'll drop you at the forecourt and collect you off a late train. Tell us your booked service when you ring and we'll work to it. We can't promise a pickup to the minute, but we can be there and waiting.

sections.heading: Airport transfers from Haslemere
sections.body:
Airport transfers are our main trade. From Haslemere:

  • Heathrow (T2–T5) — around 38 miles by road, usually via the A3 and the M25, and roughly 45 minutes to an hour when the road is clear.
  • Gatwick (North & South) — closer as the crow flies but slower going, at about 32 miles and typically an hour or so.
  • Luton, Stansted and London City — longer runs again.
  • Southampton and Portsmouth cruise terminals — regular work for us.

Every one of those times is approximate and entirely at the mercy of traffic, so we build in a sensible cushion rather than cut it fine. The car is a red Toyota Corolla saloon with a big boot, which swallows a family's airport luggage without a fight.

sections.heading: The villages around Haslemere we cover
sections.body:
Haslemere is really a cluster of places, and we pick up from all of them. Shottermill, Critchmere and Camelsdale sit to the west, Grayswood just north on the Godalming road, and Hindhead and Beacon Hill up on the ridge. We run down to Fernhurst and over toward Liphook, Petworth and Midhurst, and back the other way through Witley, Milford and Chiddingfold.

If you're up a private lane or a track with no obvious number, say so when you book and we'll find you.

sections.heading: The roads in and out — what we watch
sections.body:
The roads matter here more than most places. The A286 is the spine, running north through Milford to Godalming and south to Midhurst. The A287 comes down off Hindhead through Shottermill, the A333 carries the old A3 route through Hindhead itself, and the B2131 heads east to Petworth and west to Liphook.

The A3 is the fast way out, and it goes under the Devil's Punch Bowl through the Hindhead Tunnel — at about 1.14 miles, the longest non-estuarial road tunnel in the country. It's a lovely bit of road when it's open. When it closes, everything is pushed back onto the old surface route through Hindhead and journeys stretch out, which is one reason we ask for a little notice on early airport runs.

sections.heading: Days out, evenings and one-offs
sections.body:
Plenty of our Haslemere work isn't a flight at all. It's Black Down and the Temple of the Winds, where the National Trust car parks sit on Tennyson's Lane up Haste Hill, or the Devil's Punch Bowl, or the museum and the shops around Lion Green and the High Street.

We do evenings out, hospital and appointment runs, weddings and events, and same-day courier jobs when something needs to be somewhere and the post won't do.

sections.heading: How to book a Haslemere taxi
sections.body:
Booking is simple. Ring 01483 387 475, WhatsApp 07453 267475, or email info@chiddingfoldtaxis.co.uk. We answer 24 hours a day, seven days a week.

We don't publish a price list, because a fare from Grayswood is not a fare from Camelsdale and pretending otherwise helps nobody. Tell us where you're going and when, and we'll agree a fixed fare with you before you travel — so the number you're quoted is the number you pay.

faqs.q: How much is a taxi from Haslemere to Heathrow?
faqs.a: We don't publish fixed prices, because the fare depends on where in Haslemere you're starting, the terminal, and the time of day. Ring 01483 387 475 and we'll agree a fixed fare with you before you travel — no meter surprises at the other end.

faqs.q: Can you pick me up from Haslemere station?
faqs.a: Yes. We meet trains at the station forecourt day and night. Give us your booked service when you ring and we'll work to that arrival. We can't promise a pickup to the exact minute, but we'll be there and waiting for you.

faqs.q: Do you cover Hindhead, Shottermill and Grayswood?
faqs.a: We do — along with Critchmere, Camelsdale, Beacon Hill, Fernhurst and the lanes in between. If you're somewhere hard to find, mention it when you book and we'll sort out exactly where to meet you.

faqs.q: How long does it take to get from Haslemere to Gatwick by taxi?
faqs.a: It's about 32 miles by road and typically around an hour when traffic is kind, but that's approximate only. The M25 and the A3 can both add a lot, so we'd rather set off early than have you running for a gate.

faqs.q: Are you actually based in Haslemere?
faqs.a: No — we're honest about that. We're based in Chiddingfold, a few miles away, and Haslemere is one of our regular runs. It means a local driver setting off from nearby rather than one coming across the county, and we know the town well enough to find your door first time.
